weekNoteHtml:
How to use this page. Three movement patterns per competition event. Pick the events you want to train, then do all three patterns for that event — or cherry-pick across events. Aim for 4–6 patterns total per session. Warm up first; stop a set short if form breaks down.

Tap any movement's teal header to open it full-screen — easy to read on the gym iPad, pinch to zoom, scroll through all three exercises.

This week — comp rehearsal. Sharpening: rehearse the competition movements at comp loads, crisp and submaximal, with low volume. No maxing this week — just clean, confident reps that groove the patterns. Everything is fresh this week; read the cues, leave reps in the tank, and stay at 3 sets or fewer. Bank freshness for comp day.
testsLineHtml: No tests this week. Comp rehearsal only — light, crisp reps at competition loads, nothing to failure. Tap your name below to log a working number if you want, but leave the maxing for comp day.

patterns.name: Comp-Pace Box Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 2 min between sets · comp loads, low volume, no maxing
patterns.load: Your competition KB at competition box height
cues: Rehearse exact comp reps — same box, same bell, same rhythm.
Stop each set crisp and submaximal, never near failure.
Full sit and full stand so the pattern is dialed for comp day.
Stay relaxed and confident — this is a dress rehearsal, not a test.

patterns.name: KB Box Squat Walkthrough
patterns.rx: 2 × 5 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · slow deliberate reps to rehearse setup and stance
patterns.load: Your competition KB at competition box height
cues: Walk through your exact setup: foot position, grip, brace, then squat.
Move slow and deliberate to lock in a clean comp pattern.
Keep effort low — you are practicing, not training hard.
Reset fully between reps like you would on the comp floor.

patterns.name: Bodyweight Squat Primer
patterns.rx: 2 × 8 reps · rest 60 sec between sets · easy unloaded reps to warm the pattern
patterns.load: Bodyweight only — easy movement prep
cues: Squat to about comp depth with no load to grease the groove.
Keep it smooth and easy — finish feeling looser, not tired.
Reach your arms forward for balance as you sit and stand.
Stop well short of any fatigue.

patterns.name: Throw Technique Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 2 throws · rest 90 sec between sets · 6 crisp rehearsal throws at comp standard, submaximal
patterns.load: Dynamax ball — 4 lb (women) / 8 lb (men)
cues: Hinge and explode up, throwing the ball up and back overhead with comp-day rhythm — crisp, not maximal.
Touch the pattern to stay sharp: full setup, one breath, smooth explosive throw, then reset.
Keep effort submaximal (around 80%) — feel fast and loose, don't dig into fatigue this close to comp.
Walk through your exact comp-day routine on each rep so it's automatic on game day.

patterns.name: Bodyweight Hip Hinge — Dowel
patterns.rx: 2 × 8 reps · rest 45 sec between sets · easy groove work
patterns.load: Bodyweight holding a dowel or broomstick along your back
cues: Hold a dowel touching your head, upper back, and tailbone, then push your hips back and fold forward keeping all three contact points.
This grooves the perfect flat-back hinge you launch the throw from — keep it light and easy.
Push your hips straight back rather than bending your knees or rounding your back.
Move slowly and smoothly; this is rehearsal, not a workout.

patterns.name: Wall Slide Shoulder Prep
patterns.rx: 2 × 10 reps · rest 45 sec between sets · light overhead prep
patterns.load: Bodyweight against a wall, arms only
cues: Stand with your back and arms against a wall, then slide your arms up overhead and back down while keeping them touching the wall.
This opens up your overhead position so you reach tall and clean on comp day — keep it light and easy.
Keep your lower back flat against the wall; don't let your ribs flare as you reach up.
Move slowly and only as far up as you can while staying in contact with the wall.

patterns.name: Bench Volume @ % of Max Reps
patterns.rx: 2 sets · each set to ~40% of your max-rep test · rest 2 min between · crisp comp-speed reps
patterns.load: Your competition bench weight
cues: Your app shows your personal rep target — rehearse your comp setup and press each rep at competition speed.
Stay submaximal and crisp; touch the comp lift to stay sharp without building any fatigue. No maxing.
Stop the set as soon as the bar slows or your hips lift off the bench.

patterns.name: Scapular Wall Slide
patterns.rx: 2 × 10 reps · rest 60 sec between sets · slow and controlled
patterns.load: Bodyweight, back and arms against a wall
cues: Stand with your back, arms, and hands flat against a wall, then slide your arms up overhead and back down without losing wall contact.
This primes the shoulders and upper back for a strong bench setup — keep it slow and easy.
Only slide as high as you can while keeping your hands on the wall. No strain at all.

patterns.name: Standing Band Pull-Apart
patterns.rx: 2 × 12 reps · rest 60 sec between sets · light band
patterns.load: A light resistance band held at shoulder height
cues: Hold a light band out in front at shoulder height and pull your hands apart by squeezing your shoulder blades together.
This sets the upper-back tightness that protects your shoulders on the bench — keep the band light.
Return slowly to the front under control. Keep the effort easy and the reps smooth.

patterns.name: Max Hang Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · rest 2 min between · each hold to ~70% of your best, crisp and submaximal
patterns.load: Pull-up bar at ~70% of your best hang time — rehearse the comp setup at race standard without maxing
cues: Rehearse your exact comp-day setup: grip width, shoulder pack, breathing.
Hold submaximal and step down well before failure — no maxing this week.
Crush the bar with spread fingers so the pattern feels automatic.
Log the holds to confirm you feel sharp and fresh.

patterns.name: Comp-Grip Bar Touch
patterns.rx: 3 × 20-second hold · rest 90 sec between
patterns.load: Pull-up bar at your exact comp grip width — short, crisp 20-second holds only
cues: Take your competition grip and hold a clean, packed hang for the full 20 seconds.
Keep it short and snappy — this is rehearsal, not a fatigue set.
Breathe slow and stay quiet under the bar.
Step down on time, not at failure, to protect freshness.

patterns.name: Light Cable Row
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps · rest 75 sec between sets
patterns.load: A light cable weight you can row crisply for 10 with plenty left in the tank
cues: Row to your belly with shoulder blades pinching, then control the return.
Keep the load light and the reps sharp — touch the pattern, do not grind.
Chest up, no leaning back to muscle the weight.
Stop well short of fatigue; staying fresh is the goal.

patterns.name: Standing Slam Volume
patterns.rx: 3 × 20 sec · rest 2 min between · comp pace, submaximal
patterns.load: Your competition D-ball
cues: Rehearse race pace at about 80 percent effort — crisp and smooth, never maxing out.
Full overhead reach and a clean slam straight down on every rep.
Stop each round with reps left in the tank; this is sharpening, not testing.

patterns.name: D-Ball Catch-and-Slam Rhythm
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: Your competition D-ball
cues: Catch the bounce off the floor and flow straight into the next slam, finding a smooth rhythm.
Stay relaxed and let the ball's bounce feed your timing — don't muscle every rep.
Keep it crisp and submaximal to rehearse a clean, repeatable cadence.

patterns.name: Standing Calf Raise
patterns.rx: 3 × 12 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight or a light dumbbell in each hand
cues: Rise up onto the balls of your feet, pause at the top, then lower slowly under control.
Keep your knees straight and balance tall — hold something if you need it.
Easy supportive work to keep your ankles springy for comp.

patterns.name: 60-Sec Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 2 × 60 sec of singles at race pace · rest 2 min between sets · crisp but submaximal
patterns.load: Your jump rope, at race pace but held back from all-out
cues: Touch race speed to stay sharp, but leave a little in the tank — no maxing this week.
Rehearse your opening pace so it feels automatic on comp day.
Keep your feet quick and quiet and stop each set feeling fresh.

patterns.name: Light Standing Calf Raise
patterns.rx: 2 × 15 reps · rest 60 sec between sets · smooth and easy
patterns.load: Light DB or KB in each hand — comfortable for 15 easy reps
cues: Keep the load light and the reps smooth to stay loose, not sore.
Rise tall onto the balls of your feet and lower with control.
Stop well short of any burn — this is sharpening, not building.

patterns.name: Rhythm Pogo Taps
patterns.rx: 2 × 20 sec light pogos · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight, light springy taps
cues: Do light, springy ankle taps to keep your legs reactive and quick.
Stay tall and bounce small — quality and freshness over effort.
Keep it short and snappy and stop while you still feel light.

patterns.name: Crisp Countermovement Jump
patterns.rx: 3 × 4 jumps · rest 90 sec between sets · submaximal, snappy
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Dip quickly, throw the arms up, and jump straight up with a sharp, snappy effort — not all-out.
Land softly on the balls of your feet and bend the knees to absorb — crisp up, quiet down.
Reset fully between reps. This is sharpening, so stay submaximal and stop if landings get sloppy.

patterns.name: Short Approach Bound to Stick
patterns.rx: 3 × 4 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · one easy step, then stick
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Take one easy step in, then push off and bound forward a short, comfortable distance and stick both feet.
Keep it submaximal and rehearse a smooth, controlled landing — quality over reach.
Walk back and reset every rep. Stop the set if you can't land it softly and balanced.

patterns.name: Broad Jump — Distance Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 3 jumps · rest 2 min between sets · comp rehearsal at submax effort
patterns.load: Bodyweight · competition standard
cues: Run the full comp motion at a crisp submaximal effort — touch the pattern to stay sharp, don't max out.
Swing the arms, snap the hips, jump forward, and stick both feet cleanly every single rep.
Reset fully at the line between jumps. No maxing this week — keep it low-volume and fresh.

patterns.name: 500m Practice
patterns.rx: 2 × 250m · rest 3 min fully between · race-pace touch, submaximal
patterns.load: Rower · right at your goal 500m split — crisp and short, not maxed
cues: Touch race pace on two short pieces just to stay sharp — do not max out.
Clean, powerful strokes at your goal split, then stop while it still feels easy.
Full rest between; this is rehearsal, not a workout.

patterns.name: Scap Retraction Hold
patterns.rx: 2 × 5 slow reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A light band or cable — easy tension, sharpening only
cues: Pull the shoulder blades back and down, hold for a 3-count, then release slowly.
Keep it light and deliberate — wake the back up without fatiguing it.
No grinding; stop the moment it feels like real effort.

patterns.name: Short Bike Pickups
patterns.rx: 2 × 20 sec brisk · rest 2 min fully between · quick legs, easy effort
patterns.load: Stationary bike · a brisk but comfortable cadence, nowhere near all-out
cues: Spin the legs up quick for 20 seconds to feel snappy, then ease right back down.
Keep it light and crisp — wake the legs up without digging into fatigue.
Long full rest between — this is sharpening, not conditioning.

patterns.name: Shuttle Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 2 × 100-yd shuttle (2 × 50 yd) · rest 3–4 min · crisp submaximal race pace
patterns.load: Bodyweight · run at comp race pace but submaximal — sharp, never maxing
cues: Run two short shuttles at your race pace to rehearse the feel without digging into fatigue.
Touch each turn exactly at comp standard — controlled brake, firm plant, clean exit.
Stop while you feel fast; this is sharpening, not testing.

patterns.name: Skip for Height
patterns.rx: 3 × 20 yards of A-skips · light and rhythmic · rest 90 sec
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Skip forward driving one knee up at a time with a light, bouncy rhythm and pumping arms.
Stay tall and springy on the balls of your feet — crisp coordination, not effort.
Keep it sharp and easy to prime your mechanics for comp day.

patterns.name: Lateral Bound Touch
patterns.rx: 2 × 6 bounds (3 each side) · stick each landing · rest 90 sec
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Push off one foot to bound sideways, land softly on the other, and balance before the next.
Land under control and hold for a beat — this rehearses the side push-off of your turns at low volume.
Keep it crisp and light; no fatigue this close to comp.

patterns.name: Comp-Distance Sled Push
patterns.rx: 2 × 50 yards · rest 3 min between · submaximal comp rehearsal (not timed, never a test)
patterns.load: Competition load: 2 × 25 lb plates (women) / 2 × 45 lb plates (men)
cues: Touch the full 50 yards at comp weight twice, crisp but submaximal — stay sharp without digging in, and sled is never a timed test.
Rehearse your race feel: 45° angle, arms locked, push through the trunk, long steps with full hip extension.
No maxing and no grinding — leave plenty in the tank so you arrive fresh for comp day.

patterns.name: Walking Knee Hug to Lunge
patterns.rx: 2 × 6 reps per leg · easy pace · rest 60 sec between
patterns.load: Bodyweight only, moving down the turf
cues: Hug one knee to your chest, then step into a gentle lunge to open the hips and rehearse your stride length.
Move smooth and tall through each rep — this is a primer, not a stretch to push into.
Keep it light and easy; the goal is loose, sharp legs for comp day.

patterns.name: Pogo Hop
patterns.rx: 2 × 10 reps · rest 75 sec between · light, springy bounces
patterns.load: Bodyweight only, hopping in place off the balls of your feet
cues: Bounce lightly off the balls of your feet, springing up quickly to wake up your legs without any pounding.
Stay tall with stiff, springy ankles and a soft, quiet landing — don't sink into deep squats between hops.
Keep it short and snappy; stop while it still feels easy and bouncy.
